title: Build Dashboard Layout with Nested Routes
mission: Learn routing by building a dashboard.
description: Add React Router to your app and build a dashboard shell with sidebar, navbar, and nested pages for dashboard home, profile, and settings — plus a 404 page.
requirements: Install react-router-dom and wrap app with BrowserRouter
Create DashboardLayout with Sidebar, Navbar, and Outlet
Nested routes so sidebar and navbar persist across dashboard pages
Use Link or NavLink for sidebar navigation
Active link styling on current route
Profile and Settings pages with distinct content
Type route-related props if using useParams (optional today)
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking sidebar links changes URL and main content without full reload
Sidebar and navbar remain visible on dashboard, profile, settings
NavLink or equivalent highlights active route
hints: DashboardLayout returns div with Sidebar, Navbar, main with <Outlet />.
index route on /dashboard for default child: { index: true, element: <DashboardHome /> }.
404 as sibling: <Route path='*' element={<NotFound />} /> outside layout if needed.
Use createBrowserRouter or JSX Routes — match React Router v6 docs.

bonus.title: Route-aware navbar
requirements: Navbar title reflects current page name from route

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong route
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Blank page or router error in console
debuggingScenarios.cause: Typo in path, missing element, or parent path not matching Link to
debuggingScenarios.fix: Align Link to with route path; check relative vs absolute paths in nested config

debuggingScenarios.title: Broken navigation
debuggingScenarios.symptom: URL changes but Outlet content does not update
debuggingScenarios.cause: Outlet missing from layout or child routes not nested under layout Route
debuggingScenarios.fix: Place Outlet in layout; nest child Route elements inside parent layout route

debuggingScenarios.title: Full page reload
debuggingScenarios.symptom: White flash on navigation, network reloads document
debuggingScenarios.cause: Using <a href> instead of Link for internal routes
